channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Corsair’s PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off
item.description: On the hunt for a PC case that’s loaded with features and has a unique look? Amazon, Best Buy, and Corsair are selling the Corsair Frame 4500X RS for $119.99, a $70 discount from the usual price. The versatile case supports motherboards up to EATX, GPUs up to 460mm in length, and has a seamless […]

item.title: Qobuz overhauls its music player and adds real-time lyrics
item.description: The latest updates to Qobuz make it an even better alternative to Spotify.

item.title: Multi-turn attacks broke AI models 88% of the time — single-turn testing missed it, Cisco AI security lead warns at VB Transform 2026
item.description: When Cisco ran 6,986 multi-turn attacks against 15 flagship models, attackers who adapted across the conversation broke through as often as 88.3% of the time. Amy Chang, Cisco's head of AI threat intelligence and security research, brought that finding to the agentic security panel at VB Transform 2026; the number should worry anyone still running single-turn red-teaming programs.VentureBeat's June 2026 Pulse survey of 107 enterprise respondents explains why the room was full. More than half, 54%, have already had a confirmed agent security incident (18%) or a near-miss caught before harm (36%...

item.title: Tesla’s robotaxi promises are clashing with reality
item.description: In an earnings call yesterday, Tesla CEO Elon Musk did his best to paint a positive portrait of the company's robotaxi program. New cities are being added, more miles are being driven, and more people are experiencing Tesla's unsupervised vehicles. But Musk's typical bullishness seemed to be absent from the call, as the occasional trillionaire […]

item.title: Samsung will support the Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 with five years of updates
item.description: Samsung's new wearables will receive Wear OS updates until 2031.

item.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
item.description: Runway no longer wants to be just another AI model company. It wants to become the infrastructure layer for generative media. On Thursday, the startup launched Runway Media Router through Runway Dev, its developer platform, released earlier this month, that provides API access to a growing roster of third-party image, video and audio models alongside […]...

item.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
item.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortunate, because most enterprises cannot yet see their unit economics clearly: GPUs sit at half utilizati...

item.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
item.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whethe...

item.title: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
item.description: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the fix, but most are still building it; the field is converging on hybrid retrieval; and even as provider...

item.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
item.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to production on automated evaluation alone — with no human in the loop. The result is an evaluation gap — ...

item.title: J’ai testé la Samsung HW-Q930H : la barre de son Dolby Atmos la plus facile à recommander ?
item.description: La Samsung HW-Q930H reconduit une formule qui a fait ses preuves : une barre de son Dolby Atmos 9.1.4 livrée avec caisson et enceintes surround, le tout dans un ensemble simple à installer et particulièrement immersif. De quoi conserver sa place parmi les références du home cinéma abordable ?

item.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
item.description: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception.This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines enterprise agent orchestration: which platforms enterprises run on, what driv...

item.title: Geekbench 7 will push your computer or phone even harder for better benchmarking
item.description: Primate Labs is releasing Geekbench 7, the latest generation of its popular benchmarking tool. Geekbench 7 features new video and audio encoding / decoding tests, a redesigned multi-core test, and larger, more demanding datasets for CPUs and GPUs to crunch through. It otherwise looks and acts just like Geekbench 6, and a Pro license will […]

item.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health to everyone in the US on Thursday, allowing more people to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot. During a briefing, Ashley Alexander, OpenAI's vice president of health product, says the company's models "are now capable of reasoning at levels that are better than clinician level." […]...

item.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all U.S. users
item.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

item.title: The EU Fines Google $1 Billion for Prioritizing Its Own Services in Search
item.description: The European Commission claims that Google boosted its own apps and products to the top of search rankings to the detriment of its competitors.

item.title: Goodbye SMS or phone calls — Microsoft is making passkeys the default authentication process for businesses
item.description: Microsoft says SMS and phone call authentication isn't up to the task anymore – and urges businesses to adopt passkeys.

item.title: Xbox is testing free, ad-supported cloud gaming
item.description: Microsoft is borrowing from the GeForce Now playbook with an ad-supported cloud streaming test.

item.title: LG's latest OLED TVs will have a 'Creator Original' mode that works with Prime Video: it seems to take some of Dolby Vision 2's best features, but may arrive sooner — and unlike DV2, will be supported on LG's TVs…
item.description: LG is rolling out a new picture mode, Creator Original, to 2026 C6, G6 and W6 series TVs. It has similar features to Dolby Vision 2.

item.title: Amazon puts Luna cloud-streamed games like Fallout 4 inside Prime Video
item.description: The new strategy that Amazon gaming exec Jeff Gattis talked to The Verge about last month is getting clearer now that Prime Video has added a new Games tab on Fire TV devices. With Prime Gaming, Amazon Game Studios, and Luna now combined into one organization, it's aiming at the more casual audience of people […]

item.title: Conflict in the Strait of Hormuz has oil producers looking to spend billions to bypass it
item.description: Producers in the Persian Gulf have plans to pour billions into pipelines that would redirect supplies. Before the war in Iran, roughly 15 million barrels of Persian Gulf oil were shipped each day through the Strait of Hormuz. Within a few years, much of that oil could bypass the strait.As Iran’s chokehold over the strait drags on and oil prices surge, countries across the Gulf are planning to spend billions of dollars to build pipelines enabling them to redirect more supplies to ports along the Red Sea and the Gulf of Oman.At least seven major pipeline projects are under construction, in the p...

item.title: Après Google chez Renault, Apple Maps arrive en natif dans la future Ford électrique à moins de 30 000 $
item.description: Apple et Ford officialisent l'intégration d'Apple Maps au sein de la future plateforme électrique universelle (UEV) du constructeur américain, attendue pour 2027. L'application sera intégrée en natif, comme le fait depuis déjà quelques années Google avec Maps chez certains constructeurs comme Renault ou encore Volvo.

item.title: Tesla’s robotaxis are moving in reverse
item.description: The number of paid robotaxi miles traveled fell 36% in the second quarter, despite expanding to new cities, according to Tesla's own figures.

item.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
item.description: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

item.title: Xbox Cloud Gaming lance une formule gratuite financée par la publicité
item.description: Xbox ouvre enfin son cloud gaming gratuit supporté par la publicité. En test auprès d'une partie de ses utilisateurs, cette formule limite cependant le temps de jeu des joueurs.

item.title: Zach Cregger's Resident Evil just got a new trailer and eagle-eyed fans have already spotted some video game easter eggs
item.description: A new trailer for Zach Cregger's Resident Evil film has been released, and it's filled with easter eggs from the beloved video game series.

item.title: Duda expands its AI stack to bridge the gap between fast website generation and enterprise-grade security
item.description: AI tools built specifically with agencies in mind

item.title: NordVPN lance HangUp, une application gratuite contre les appels frauduleux sur Android
item.description: HangUp est une application antispam créée par NordVPN. Elle est gratuite et uniquement disponible sur Android.

item.title: Ring has upgraded two of its cameras and the new peephole model looks like it'll be perfect for apartment-dwellers —its no-drill installation won't damage your door, so it's ideal for renters
item.description: Ring has updated its Outdoor Camera and Peephole Camera with 2K resolution and 6x enhanced zoom.

item.title: Samsung just fixed the foldable phone — now Apple can make it cool
item.description: The new Samsung Z Fold 8 Ultra is the best folding phone I've seen, but I'm waiting to see what Apple does in the same space.

item.title: Amazon Luna boss thinks rising hardware prices could push more hardcore gamers to cloud gaming — 'You're going to see more people that are priced out of the market'
item.description: Amazon's GM of gaming thinks the rising cost of consoles and PC components could push more hardcore gamers towards cloud streaming.

item.title: Potensic ATOM 3 : ce nouveau drone 4K grand public est déjà en promo à moins de 350 €
item.description: Compact, léger et capable de filmer en 4K, le Potensic ATOM 3 est le nouveau drone grand public de la marque chinoise. Et il est déjà proposé à un prix assez attractif : 349,99 euros au lieu de 379,99 euros grâce à un coupon sur Amazon.

item.title: ‘Our nation depends on our men and women who fight on the front lines’: Nvidia donates DGX GB300 AI supercomputer to US Naval Postgraduate School to train AI leaders, run high-fidelity simulations, and create naval digital twin environments
item.description: The supercomputer will now be used across a range of applications, from simulations and digital twins, to weather prediction and cybersecurity

item.title: The RAM crisis just forced Framework into a nasty price hike — and Apple's rumored solution to the memory crunch is renting Macs to cash-strapped consumers
item.description: Apple's plan to sidestep the RAM crisis could be renting Macs, iPhones and iPads to consumers.

item.title: Google will now let you sign in to your account with a selfie video
item.description: The tech giant says selfie videos give users more options to sign in if they're ever locked out or don't have access to their usual phone or computer.

item.title: Oubliez les mastodontes : avec 300 km d’autonomie, la véritable Smart arrive enfin !
item.description: Smart prépare un retour aux sources avec la nouvelle #2. Prévue pour le Mondial de l’Automobile à Paris, la mini citadine électrique présentera une autonomie de 300 km dans un emballage mimi tiré du concept présenté en avril.

item.title: Microsoft is testing free, ad-supported cloud gaming for Xbox Insiders
item.description: Xbox Insiders can stream games from their libraries for free in ad-supported streaming sessions starting today. The free streaming sessions are limited to games you already own, and sessions are capped at one hour. However, ads will only play "before sessions begin." It's also optional - you won't need to watch ads if you have […]

item.title: An OpenAI model went rogue on the internet and stole test answers
item.description: The unprecedented incident took place during a safety test at OpenAI. Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.

item.title: Valve launches two big Steam updates, and one of them arrives at a perfect time
item.description: Valve continues to make the user experience on Steam easier via numerous updates, and two recent updates serve as major overhauls.

item.title: Flock cameras spotted hiding inside speed signs from a growing backlash — as Wisconsin officials resort to covering ALPRs with trash bags after company refuses to take them down
item.description: Flock cameras are still getting heated backlash, and now these ALPR systems are being hidden inside speed signs.

item.title: Google gets a $1 billion fine by the European Union over its Play app
item.description: It’s the latest big tech crackdown by EU antitrust regulators. The European Union on Thursday hit hit Google with a fine of 890 million euros ($1 billion) after it said the technology behemoth broke digital antitrust regulations by setting up Google Play and its ubiquitous search engine to corral consumers towards its own services and apps to the detriment of competitors.It was the latest major crackdown on Big Tech by Brussels, which has led the world in reining in some of the world’s largest companies from Silicon Valley to Beijing.It has done so despite the risk of incurring the wrath of Pr...

item.title: Apple Maps will power navigation in Ford's upcoming slate of EVs
item.description: The car manufacturer is planning a big push into electric vehicles.

item.title: An AI now judges every move Rubrik's agents make, its AI chief said at VB Transform 2026 — but no one's measured if the judge is right
item.description: At a CISO roundtable organized by Anthropic's chief information security officer, Dev Rishi asked a simple question: Did everyone in the room have their AI governance and security policies written down? Every hand went up — about 14 people, by his count. His follow-up, about how anyone actually enforces those policies in practice, got a different response. "And everybody chuckled," Rishi, the GM of AI at Rubrik, recalled at VB Transform 2026 fireside chat in Menlo Park. "It was like the dirty secret in the room that everyone has these policies, but no way to actually make them real."“Our found...

item.title: Tesla FSD bloqué en France : c’est quoi la « vitesse maximale contextuelle » qui inquiète les autorités ?
item.description: La France vient de dire non au FSD (Conduite entièrement automatique) de Tesla, et l'un des reproches du ministre des Transports tient à une seule fonction, capable de faire rouler la voiture au-dessus de la limite. Elle porte un nom obscur : la vitesse maximale contextuelle.

item.title: Does charging your phone in your car waste gas?
item.description: Charing your phone in the car may technically use energy, but not enough to give a second thought.

item.title: Pourquoi un point bleu est apparu dans la barre d’état d’Android
item.description: Google a fait évoluer la gestion de la vie privée sur Android. Après un indicateur vert, un point bleu s’affiche désormais dans la barre d’état. Voici ce que change cet affichage et comment fonctionnent les témoins de couleur.
